techniques.name: Execute Non-Owner Instruction
techniques.notes: Gateway bypass

techniques.name: Infer Implicit Authority
techniques.notes: Replay attack — no deduplication

techniques.name: Propagate Spoofed Authority at Scale
techniques.notes: 50 agents bulk-granted — no distribution controls

techniques.name: Expand Task Scope Autonomously
techniques.notes: Overly broad capability patterns

techniques.name: Perform Unvalidated Bulk Operations
techniques.notes: 100 individual deletes approved — no aggregate detection

techniques.name: Obscure Objective Through Delegation
techniques.notes: Read+send compose to exfiltration — no cross-agent correlation

techniques.name: Exploit Tool-Chain Composition
techniques.notes: Expired capability enforcement

techniques.name: Perform Irreversible Destructive Action
techniques.notes: rm -rf approved same as benign — no proportionality gate

techniques.name: Trigger Cascading System Changes
techniques.notes: Behavioral anomaly detects severity trajectory shift (file_read baseline → shell_exec escalation). Historical rate accumulation validated over 40+ sequential requests.

techniques.name: Exfiltrate Context-Scoped Data
techniques.notes: Data exfiltration requires actual data flow between agent and external systems — not exercisable at engine layer

techniques.name: Leak Cross-Session or Persistent Data
techniques.notes: Cross-session data leakage requires persistent agent memory across sessions — not exercisable at engine layer

techniques.name: Cross-Domain Secret Leakage
techniques.notes: Cross-domain leakage requires multi-domain deployment with trust boundaries — not exercisable at engine layer

techniques.name: Report False Task Completion
techniques.notes: Audit trail records denials, escalations, and tool execution failures. Agent cannot claim success when action was denied or tool failed (execution_failed audit record).

techniques.name: Fabricate Action Attribution
techniques.notes: No authentication — any caller can submit as any agent_id

techniques.name: Suppress or Omit Execution Failure
techniques.notes: Audit records approval but not execution failure

techniques.name: Execute Recursive Invocation Loops
techniques.notes: 50 recursive governance evaluations — no depth limit

techniques.name: Consume Unbounded External Resources
techniques.notes: No parameter size limits

techniques.name: Spoof Agent Identity
techniques.notes: Concurrent flood test

techniques.name: Inject Malicious Delegation Chains
techniques.notes: Agent A creates proxy as Agent B — no per-hop auth

techniques.name: Induce Cross-Agent Behavioral Drift
techniques.notes: Behavioral drift requires agent-level longitudinal testing with shared context — not exercisable at engine layer

techniques.name: Induce Unsafe Consensus
techniques.notes: 10 identical sensitive requests — no convergence detection

techniques.name: Poison Persistent Memory
techniques.notes: Malicious metadata stored verbatim in audit trail

techniques.name: Corrupt Governance or Policy State
techniques.notes: No auth on policy registration

techniques.name: Operate Outside Monitoring Boundaries
techniques.notes: Bypass = operating outside monitoring

techniques.name: Obfuscate Action Traceability
techniques.notes: Batch timestamp collision confirms T9002 — implementation defect validates the technique, does not warrant new subtechnique per MITRE criteria

techniques.name: Exploit Pseudo-Filesystem Capabilities
techniques.notes: Path traversal via fnmatch

techniques.name: Establish Persistence via Environment Auto-Execution
techniques.notes: Gateway blocks FILE_WRITE to git hooks, shell init, CI/CD configs, Dockerfiles, package lifecycle scripts, and IDE task files. Path traversal normalization validated.

techniques.name: Inject Persistent Agent Instructions
techniques.notes: Gateway blocks FILE_WRITE to CLAUDE.md, .claude/*, .cursorrules, copilot-instructions.md, .windsurfrules, .clinerules. Path traversal normalization validated. FILE_READ allowed (read is safe, only writes are T10003).

techniques.name: Exploit Governance-Runtime Parser Divergence
techniques.notes: Shell metacharacters in targets approved by fnmatch — governance sees one string, shell would execute multiple commands

atm1_attack_vectors.name: Protocol-level attacks (MITM, message injection, replay, token theft)
Policy-layer attacks (evasion, bypass, tampering, authorization bypass)
Identity & authentication attacks (spoofing, lateral movement, credential harvesting)
Timing & side-channel attacks (policy eval timing, risk scoring inference)
Supply-chain & dependency attacks (dependency poisoning, build artifact tampering)
Distributed & coordinated attacks (coordinated low-risk, slow-burn, federation signal)

atm1_security_properties.name: Actor Attribution — every action attributable to authenticated actor
atm1_security_properties.notes: No authentication on agent_id — SP-2 partially violated

atm1_security_properties.name: Policy Immutability Window — active policy cannot change during execution
atm1_security_properties.notes: TOCTOU fixed by unified eval lock (BT-005)

atm1_security_properties.name: Capability Authorization Binding — execution requires prior authorization
Audit Completeness & Append-Only — every decision captured, no retroactive modification

deferred_findings.title: object.__setattr__ bypass
deferred_findings.risk: Python C-level slot access bypasses custom __setattr__

deferred_findings.title: Module attribute replacement
deferred_findings.risk: Entire scoring tables replaceable at module level

deferred_findings.title: Silent evidence replacement
deferred_findings.risk: Corrupted audit JSON replaced with empty dict

deferred_findings.title: Parameter semantic analysis
deferred_findings.risk: Risk engine scores target string, not parameters
